stories.title: How Google’s A.I. Search Is Imperiling the Open Web
stories.simple_summary: Google's AI search tool is changing how the internet works—it answers questions directly instead of sending people to other websites, which worries people who run those websites.
stories.why_it_matters: If fewer people click through to websites, small business owners who rely on Google search traffic could see fewer visitors to their sites.

stories.title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
stories.simple_summary: Anthropic (an AI company) paid $1.5 billion to settle a lawsuit about using copyrighted writing to train their AI, though this doesn't fully solve the bigger copyright problem.
stories.why_it_matters: This shows AI companies may have to pay for using people's creative work, which could affect how AI tools are built going forward.

stories.title: CNBC's The China Connection newsletter: The AI consumer bet might surprise you
stories.simple_summary: This headline doesn't give enough detail to explain what it's about.

stories.title: Head of US AI safety agency resigns
stories.simple_summary: The person leading the U.S. government's AI safety efforts has quit.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and people focused on AI policy—nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Trump’s latest AI czar has already resigned
stories.simple_summary: Another person has quit as the head of the government's AI standards office. This job has had a lot of turnover.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and people tracking government leadership—nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Here are the 30,000 songs Sony is suing Udio’s AI music generator over
stories.simple_summary: Sony is suing an AI music-making tool for illegally copying more than 30,000 of its songs, including famous ones.
stories.why_it_matters: This signals that AI tools may not be able to freely use famous music—important if you were thinking AI could create music for your business for free.

stories.title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
stories.simple_summary: Google is designing a new computer chip to make its Gemini AI run faster and cheaper.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and engineers—it's an internal improvement that won't directly affect you.

stories.title: SpaceX in your index fund, explained
stories.simple_summary: This article is about how SpaceX might get added to a popular investment fund—not related to AI.
stories.why_it_matters: This isn't about AI and mostly matters to people managing investment portfolios.

stories.title: AI’s most important protocol is getting a little bit easier to use
stories.simple_summary: A technical system that AI uses behind the scenes is being redesigned to work more simply.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to AI engineers and companies building AI tools—nothing you need to know.

stories.title: Opinion | Powerful AI models are being given away for free. It was inevitable.
stories.simple_summary: Powerful AI models are being released for free, and this was bound to happen.
stories.why_it_matters: This means more AI tools will become available to regular people and small businesses without expensive fees.

stories.title: X relaunches a rebuilt Android app after year-long effort
stories.simple_summary: X (formerly Twitter) finished rebuilding its app for Android phones after a year of work.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use X on an Android phone, the app should work better—nothing urgent you need to do.

stories.title: OpenAI is scared of open-weight models. Should the US be?
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI is worried about free AI models (especially ones made in China) that anyone can use and modify.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and people watching AI business competition—nothing you need to act on.
